publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-11-17 18:12:16 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Transcripts of Herring-Curtiss Company vs. Glenn Curtiss et al., briefs, exhibits, correspondence, and other papers pertaining to this lawsuit, in which William Maloney was one of the principal trial lawyers, and to related litigation; incorporation papers, minutes, resolutions, stock certificates, legal documents, correspondence, and other items (1909-1031) relating to the business affairs of the company; Augustus Herring's scrapbook (1896-1909) and notebook (ca. 1903-1910) dealing with his role in airplane and glider development and his correspondence with the United States Patent Office (1909-1913); blueprints, photographs, pamphlets, and clippings relating to general aspects of aviation development.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Transcripts of Herring-Curtiss Company vs. Glenn Curtiss et al., briefs, exhibits, correspondence, and other papers pertaining to this lawsuit, in which William Maloney was one of the principal trial lawyers, and to related litigation; incorporation papers, minutes, resolutions, stock certificates, legal documents, correspondence, and other items (1909-1031) relating to the business affairs of the company; Augustus Herring's scrapbook (1896-1909) and notebook (ca. 1903-1910) dealing with his role in airplane and glider development and his correspondence with the United States Patent Office (1909-1913); blueprints, photographs, pamphlets, and clippings relating to general aspects of aviation development.

p.emph: Due to fragility, instead of using the 1910 Datebook and 1896-1910 Scrapbook found in Box 3, please use the Microfilm [reel 1 for Datebook and reel 3 for Scrapbook].

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Augustus Moore Herring papers, #1990. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to 1910 Datebook and 1896-1910 Scrapbook in Box 3 restriced to the permission of the curator.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Due to fragility,researchers must use microfilm of the 1910 Datebook and 1896-1910 Scrapbook in Box 3.

unittitle.extref: Link to a copy of the printed finding aid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Relating to airplane development, financial transactions of the Herring-Curtiss Company and the Herring-Curtiss vs Glenn Curtiss suit 1894-1931

did.unittitle: August Herring to James Gordon Bennett

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Bennett of the New York Herald, requesting financial aid for aviation research and explaining current progress and innovations. Carbon copies. Also on mf. (neg & pos.)

did.unittitle: Application to United States Patent Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Orville and Wilbur Wright for improvements in flying machines

did.unittitle: U.S. Patent Office to Augustus Herring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter concerning Herring's application for patent and pointing out inadequacies in Herring's blueprints

did.unittitle: Herring to U. S. Patent Office

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correcting blueprint errors and submitting new papers. Carbon copy

did.unittitle: Standard Audit Company vs Herring-Curtiss Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summons, city of New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters concerning financial transactions of the Herring-Curtiss Company and condemning Glenn Curtiss Business policies. Carbon copies

did.unittitle: U. S. Patent Office to Augustus Herring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter containing a partial rejection of Herring patent claims

did.unittitle: U. S. Patent Office to Augustus Herring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter containing suggestions to Herring on airplane patent

did.unittitle: U. S. Patent Office to Augustus Herring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters replying to Herring's application for patent amendment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Herring vs Bernhard Montgomery Beskow

did.unittitle: Denial of Herring's petition of Interference

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon copy of Herring's proposed amendment to June 18, 1910 Patent Application

did.unittitle: Statements relating to the Herring-Beskow case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon copies of Herring's proposed amendments to his June 18, 1910 patent application

did.unittitle: U. S. Patent Office to Augustus Herring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Herring rejecting his proposed amendment of Nov. 12, 1912

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Carbon copies of Herring's new proposed amendments to his June 18, 1910 patent application

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Of financial position and character of Glenn Curtiss. No author given.

did.unittitle: Resolution adopted by stockholders of Herring-Curtiss Company
Resolution adopted by Board of Directors of Herring-Curtiss Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See attached addenda sheet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stock holders in the Herring-Curtiss Company, original issue and changes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An estimate (by Herring) of financial losses due to the Wright brothers injunction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of a meeting of the Board of Directors of the Herring-Curtiss Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Directors of the Herring-Curtiss Company directed to Monroe Wheeler, Glenn Curtiss, and Thomas Baldwin

did.unittitle: Series II. Legal Documents and Magazine Articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes loose unbound printed material concerning aviation development 1891-1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters - patent from U. S. A. to Augustus M. Herring for anti-drip nose for pitchers

did.unittitle: Warranty deed from Jefferis to Brakman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From John H. Jefferis of Rochester to Abraham L. Brakman and wife Caroline Brakman of Rochester for tract of land in Rochester

did.unittitle: Warranty deed from Brakman to Lisk

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Abraham L. Brakman and wife Caroline Brakman of Rochester to Alice Elizabeth Lisk of same place for same tract of land in Rochester as that described in deed 2 #1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bond on Alice Elizabeth Lisk of Rochester to Caroline Brakman of same place for sum of money

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mortgage for indebtedness of A. E. Lisk to Caroline Brakman in connection with land conveyed by deed 2 #2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bond on A. E. Lisk to the Albany City Savings Institution for sum of money in connection with her indebtedness

did.unittitle: Sworn statement by Hugh L. Willoughby

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Willoughby of Servall's Point, Florida, a stockholder in The Herring-Curtiss Company, concerning stock issued to him

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fifteen copies of Gas Power magazine for July, Sept., Oct., 1903; Feb., March, June, July, Aug., Sept., Oct., Nov., Dec., 1904; Jan., Feb., June 1905

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pictures and article relating to the International Balloon Race, Berlin, Germany

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Packet containing the minutes of the first meeting of the Herring-Curtiss Company, drafts of agreements entered into between the various members of the Herring-Curtiss Company, terms of incorporation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing newspaper clippings, magazine articles, and pictures relating to Herring's role in airplane and glider development, Marked "Scrapbook I" (fragile) Also on mf. reel 3

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dealing with the Herring-Curtiss Company suit against Curtiss.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appears to have been prepared by Herring

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Herring Vs Curtiss et. Al. for $5,000,000 pp. 1-71

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing daily entries for 1910. Also available on mf. Reel 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing chronology of agreements, documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Conquest of the Air"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report #82 by A. F. Zahm

did.unittitle: Series III. Books and Printed Items
The Wright Company vs. The Herring Company, United States Circuit Court of Appeals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Western District of New York, Transcript of Record, The Wright Company vs. The Herring Company. Bound Volume

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. I, U. S. Circuit Court of Appeals, Western District of New York, Testimony, Defendant's Exhibits, Stipulations and Agreements. Bound Volume (cover missing) 617 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound Volume (binding broken)

unittitle.title: He Was Given Up For Dead

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An Address on the Occasion of the Twenty-Fifth Anniversary of the Babson Organization by Dr. George W. Coleman. Story of Roger W. Babson. Pamphlet 24pp.

unittitle.title: William Randolph Hearst on the Prosperity Loan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reprint of editorial columns of the New York American and other Hearst newspapers. Pamphlet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Detail of conn. Rod. 8" x 12 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blueprint 10 1/2" x 26" (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 28" x 30" (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 18" x 12" - oilcloth type paper (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (motorcycle) 13" x 24" (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10" x 13 1/2" (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10" x 14" (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 1/2" x 21" (unavailable)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oilcloth type 10" x 14"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Oilcloth type paper 17" x 24"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Made for government contract, A. M. Herring 9" x 13"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: G. H. Curtiss Mfg. Co., Hammondsport, N.Y. 2 copies 10 1/2" x 14"

did.unittitle: O/S drawing of radial engine Unlabeled

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 15" x 22" Mapcase Folder 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright 1914 by Mentor Association 7" x 9 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright 1914 by Mentor 7" x 9 1/2"

did.unittitle: Moisant circling the Statue of Liberty

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7" x 9 1/2" (These first 3 photos are the third, fifth and sixth monographs in the Mentor Reading Course copyright 1914 by the Mentor Association, Inc.)

did.unittitle: Printed Postcard Photo of monoplane passing over farmers in a field

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attached is a subscription reply postcard for joining Aeronautics magazine, N.Y.C. 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

did.unittitle: French colored picture postcard showing a little girl flying a small French airplane

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sent to A. M. Herring from C.L.S., Rome, Italy 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Feb 10, 1910 order for an aeroplane from Haag's Shows, Montgomery, Alabama and reply from Curtiss-Hammond Co., Hammondsport, N.Y. 4 3/4" x 6 3/4"

did.unittitle: Matte Photo of Junebug in flight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright by H.M. Benner 1908 5" x 8"

did.unittitle: Matte postcard photo of Junebug in flight

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright by H.M. Benner 1908 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

did.unittitle: Matte postcard photo titled "Curtiss Aeroplane Ready for a Flight" from frozen lake

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copyright by H.O. Benner 1910 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scene somewhat similar to #9 4 1/2" x 6 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Whitewing biplane on ground with people around it 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probable Silver Dart sitting on ground 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Probable Silver Dart being pulled by men across a snowy field 4" x 6 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Herring-Burgess Flying Fish (per E.E. Husting, 6-74) 4 1/2" x 6 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Loon at waters edge ready to be tried out by McCurdy 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Loon running on the water. First attempts at making a hydroaeroplane by Curtiss and Associates 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The name Charles Marvin, Elmira, N.Y. written on the reverse 3 3/4" x 6"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grahame White's (illegible) machine. Arthur Powell, York Studio, Torquay, Devon, England 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

Glossy photo of biplane in hanger near water

scopecontent.p: Large crowd gathered on beach outside 8" x 10"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sign overhead reads, Bettendorf Axle Co. 8" x 10"

did.unittitle: Glossy photo identified as "The First Herring Curtiss Machine (biplane)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: At Morris Park, N.Y. Copyright by Underwood and Underwood N.Y. 1909 3 1/2" x 4 1/2"

did.unittitle: Semi-glossy photo with Herring biplane suspended from ceiling

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: As part of an exhibit and in left of photo a sign "1897 Herring and Arnot Gliding Machine" 4 3/4" x 6 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spooner and Wells, Inc., Photographers, N.Y.C. In 2 pieces 1 1/2" x 7 1/2" and 4 1/4" x 7 1/2"

did.unittitle: Matte photo identified as First Herring Curtiss 25 HP Motors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Which were built in 1907-1908 7" x 9"

did.unittitle: Glossy print identified as 8 cylinder motor of Herring Curtiss Rheims machine
Matte photo of the porch and sign on the Mineola Hotel
Printed photocard of Schloss von der Gartenseite. Schnetzinger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The name Judge Stevens typed on reverse side 3 1/2" x 5 1/2"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper Clippings 1901, 1906, 1908-1922
